accuracy_212_pairs.basis: SMT-based verification on 212 function pairs (52 core + 130 expanded + 19 memory + 11 C2Rust-realistic)
accuracy_212_pairs.methodology: Each pair encoded via σ-bridge into QF_BV/QF_ABV constraints, solved with Z3
limitations: Struct category achieves 0% due to parser limitations on typedef struct
Loop analysis is bounded model checking (BMC) at K=32
Memory category achieves 28.6% — limited by frontend support for pointer types in function signatures
Overall accuracy reflects inclusion of categories beyond the supported fragment
IR lowering failures on struct-heavy, macro-generated, or generic code account for majority of errors

core_fragment_accuracy.value: 83.3% on arithmetic, 100% on shift, 60% on cast/division
core_fragment_accuracy.basis: Core supported fragment: arithmetic, bitwise, division, shift, cast, error handling

memory_accuracy.basis: 19 memory/pointer benchmark pairs using QF_ABV array theory extension

c2rust_realistic_accuracy.basis: 11 pairs approximating real C2Rust transpiler output patterns

cegar_convergence.basis: CEGAR loop with GPT-4.1-nano, 5 iterations each, 20 UB-heavy functions
cegar_convergence.methodology: Improved repair hints with UB-specific guidance and conditional equivalence mode
cegar_convergence.improvement: Up from 0% on UB functions in prior version

core_32_pair_accuracy.basis: 32 hand-crafted pairs where all pipeline stages succeed

k_sensitivity.conclusion: K=32 default is empirically justified; no additional divergences found at higher K

real_world_c_libraries.basis: 15 function pairs from 5 open-source C libraries

includes: Integer arithmetic (i8-i64, u8-u64) with overflow semantics
Bitwise operations (AND, OR, XOR, NOT, SHL, SHR)
Control flow (if/else, bounded loops via BMC at K=32, switch/match)
Type casts (widening, narrowing, sign change, float conversion)
Struct types (field access, construction, nested structs)
Enum/tagged union types (discriminant extraction, variant construction)
Pointer/memory operations via QF_ABV: alloca, load, store, GEP, malloc/free, memcpy/memset
Tree-sitter-based parsing (tree-sitter-c 0.24, tree-sitter-rust 0.24) with hand-written fallback
Points-to analysis (Andersen-style) + Rust ownership non-aliasing axioms + TBAA
excludes: Typedef struct in C parser (parser limitation)

honest_scoping.loop_analysis: Bounded model checking (BMC) at depth K=32, NOT full verification. Exact for bounded loops, best-effort for unbounded. An UNSAT result at depth K guarantees equivalence for all executions of ≤ K iterations.
honest_scoping.memory_model: Byte-addressable array model (QF_ABV). Tracks alloca/store/load/GEP with SSA versioning. Allocation non-overlap and alignment enforced via Z3 constraints. Does not model garbage collection, custom allocators, or memory-mapped I/O.
honest_scoping.struct_encoding: Flat bitvector concatenation — correct for value semantics, does not model pointer-to-struct or heap-allocated structs
honest_scoping.enum_encoding: Tagged bitvector union — correct for discriminant + payload, does not model enum layout optimizations (niche filling)
honest_scoping.float_encoding: IEEE 754 via Z3 QF_FP — correct for standard operations, does not model platform-specific behavior or -ffast-math
honest_scoping.cegar_conditional_equivalence: CEGAR mode accepts translations where outputs match on all well-defined inputs, recognizing UB-only divergences as correct wrapping translations. This is sound: if C has UB on input x, any Rust behavior on x is acceptable.

conditional_soundness.statement: For functions in F: UNSAT ⟹ ∀x∈D_¬UB. f_C(x) = f_R(x)
conditions: Functions must be in supported fragment F (now including memory operations)
Loops must have ≤ K iterations for exact results
Memory operations must be within allocated bounds

counterexample_correctness.statement: SAT with model x₀ ⟹ either output divergence or C UB

decidability.statement: For F, the verification problem is decidable (QF_BV + QF_ABV + BMC)

memory_soundness_extension.statement: For functions in F_M (F extended with alloca/load/store/GEP): UNSAT ⟹ ∀x∈D_¬UB_M. f_C(x) = f_R(x)
conditions: D_¬UB_M excludes inputs causing out-of-bounds access, null dereference, or use-after-free
Memory operations must be on statically deterministic allocation sizes
memcpy/memset bounded to 64 bytes in unrolling

sigma_bridge_formal_semantics.ub_encoding: C UB is encoded as havoc: when an operation triggers UB (detected via overflow/shift/division predicates), the C-side result is replaced with an unconstrained symbolic bitvector. This models the fact that a C compiler may produce ANY value for UB operations.
sigma_bridge_formal_semantics.overflow_mode_ub: For OverflowMode.UB: emit ub_flag = bvsadd_overflow(a,b); result = ite(ub_flag, fresh_unconstrained_bv, bvadd(a,b)). The ub_flag is added to the C-side assumptions list.
sigma_bridge_formal_semantics.overflow_mode_wrap: For OverflowMode.WRAP: emit result = bvadd(a,b). No assumptions added.
sigma_bridge_formal_semantics.shift_model_ub: For ShiftModel.UB: if shift_amount ≥ bitwidth, flag as UB (havoc result). For ShiftModel.MASK: emit shift_amount & (bitwidth-1).
sigma_bridge_formal_semantics.division_model: For DivisionModel.UB: flag division by zero and INT_MIN/-1 as UB. For DivisionModel.PANIC: these are divergent from C's UB.
sigma_bridge_formal_semantics.implementation_defined_behavior: C implementation-defined behaviors (int width, char signedness, struct padding) are currently fixed: int=32 bits, char=signed, padding=standard ABI alignment. Future work: parameterize via σ_C configuration.

sigma_bridge_coercion_catalog.verification: Boundary-value testing on all critical inputs
